items.title: AFL’s first publicly gay player calls for respect in Crows-Pies match

children.content: Four months after becoming the first current or former AFL player to publicly come out as gay, Leigh Ryswyk is urging fans not to boo the Collingwood player targeted by Crow Izak Rankine’s homophobic slur.

items.title: SA government hits 'pause' on compulsory acquisition of popular restaurant

children.content: The state government last week announced a $61.1 million preschool and primary school would be built on a 5.5-hectare parcel of land in Mount Barker's south-east, including on the site of popular Thai restaurant Wok The Duck.

image.altText: A man stands in front of a artistically graffitied wall with his arms crossed.

items.title: Unlawful excavation of river cliff disturbs Aboriginal ancestral remains

children.content: A cliff site in South Australia's Riverland, home to Aboriginal ancestral remains, has been excavated without authorisation.

image.altText: An Aboriginal woman in front of a damaged cliff face.

items.title: Weightlifter puts 'best foot forward' for Comm Games

children.content: Kiana Elliott thought gymnastics would be her pathway to the podium, but turned to weightlifting after an injury forced her to retire.

items.title: Whyalla workers injured at steelworks' blast furnace

children.content: Three Whyalla steelworks employees have been treated for injuries suffered during attempts to restart the site's troubled blast furnace, the ABC understands.

image.altText: A worker at the face of the blast furnace at the Whyalla steelworks.

items.title: Rain cancels outback reunions and events for second year

children.content: Flood-damaged roads and soggy racetracks mean some of the outback's favourite events are being cancelled and keeping tourists away.

image.altText: A young girl riding a horse, with a woman leading the horse

items.title: Gas tax, gambling and AI at stake at the Labor conference

children.content: Labor members and unions are gearing up for fights over gambling, gas and AI at the party's national conference later this week.

image.altText: Anthony Albanese

items.title: Anxiety as Australia issues new travel advice for Middle East countries

children.content: As conflict between the United States and Iran escalates once again, the Department of Foreign Affairs and Trade has issued a warning to "reconsider" travel to several more countries in the region.

image.altText: Woman at airport

items.title: 'Nervous' investors retreat from housing market as downturn broadens

children.content: Australia's property market has entered a downturn after both house and unit prices fell for the first time in more than three years. Despite the downturn, house prices remained at record highs in the South Australian capital, as well as Brisbane, Perth and Hobart.

items.title: Minister says Australian IS-linked 'Doctor Jihad' can 'rot' in Iraqi jail

children.content: Perth paediatrician Tareq Kamleh went to Syria to join the Islamic State group in 2015 and has, until now, been presumed dead.

image.altText: Dr Tareq Kamleh

items.title: Inquest to determine whether man killed his mother and staged her death

children.content: An inquest is investigating whether a man killed his mother before placing her in the driver's seat of her car to stage that she was behind the wheel during a crash.

image.altText: A man with brown hair and beard smiles for the camera inside a family kitchen

items.title: Tony Modra shares post-surgery photos after truck crash

items.title: From Mumbai to New York, young Indians gather in unprecedented movement

children.content: A student who started a youth uprising across India is fast becoming the biggest challenge to Prime Minister Narendra Modi's 12-year reign.

items.title: French modelling scout with links to Epstein found dead, prosecutors say

items.title: How we got to Glasgow 2026 and what the Commonwealth Games will look like

children.content: The bumpy road to Glasgow 2026 has produced a Commonwealth Games that will look a little different than the ones that came before.

articles.title: Your guide to every day of the Commonwealth Games in Australian time

children.content: With 10 sports crammed into 10 full days of action in Glasgow, it may be hard to keep track of what's on and when at the Commonwealth Games. So, here's the full schedule of every day of the 2026 Glasgow Commonwealth Games with all times and days in AEST.

items.title: Nintendo raised prices due to US tariffs. Now, it doesn't want to pass on refunds

items.title: Data company urged to be 'good' after water plan slip reveals intentions

items.title: Rural suburb flagged as home for proposed $40b 'hyperscale' data centre

children.content: A proposed massive new data centre would require more than six times the total demand of the Darwin and Katherine electricity grids to operate and be powered by gas piped direct from the Northern Territory's Beetaloo Basin.

articles.title: 'We do not want to highlight this yet': AI data centre's FAQ slip-up
